note: Synthetic append-only log for the refund process from the intake record. After the open questions were answered, the required SOX controls were confirmed and a pilot launched: support agents may issue goodwill credits up to $50 directly (fully logged in NetSuite for audit); the finance/controller chain is retained above $50, and the director threshold above $500 is unchanged. Baseline cycle time ~7 business days; target ~1. Two monthly readings after pilot launch.

entries.note: First reading after pilot launch. Small credits (<= $50) now issued at first touch; the week-plus cases are now only the larger, control-required refunds.

adoption_rate.basis: credits issued via agent self-service / all eligible (<= $50) credits

adoption_reuse.reusability_rationale: Pattern (thresholded self-service + retained control above the line) is portable, but only Support has adopted it so far.

entries.note: Approaching target. The redundant support-lead pre-check was removed after confirming it duplicated finance's control (no SOX control lost); the controller sign-off was retained as a required control.

adoption_rate.basis: credits issued via agent self-service / all eligible (<= $50) credits

adoption_reuse.reusability_rationale: A second team adopted the thresholded self-service + retained-control pattern with only a policy/threshold change. Candidate to scale (see process-improve).

readout: cycle_time: 1.4 business_days as of 2026-09-30 - DOWN 80% vs baseline (7 -> 1.4), 93% of the way to the 1-day target. adoption_rate: 86% (rising -> sustained), reuse_count: 1 other team. Reusability now 'yes' -> flag for scale. Governance preserved: required SOX/controller control retained; only a duplicated pre-check was removed.
